titlestmt.author: Compiled by Maria A. Keating, Sarah K. Gordon

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-04-08 18:32:16 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Personal material and material relating to Sarah B. Cooper's work in Bible Schools, Kindergartens, and Women's Organizations.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: Compiled by:
defitem.item: Maria A. Keating, Sarah K. Gordon

eventgrp.event: Sarah Ingersoll born in Cazenovia, New York
Sarah teaches District summer school in Eagle Village, New York
Begins Bible Class for the community
Sarah attends Cazenovia Seminary
Meets Halsey Fenimore Cooper
Sarah attends Troy Female Seminary of Emma Willard, studies Music and Foreign Language
Works as a governess for the Schley family in Augusta, Georgia
Sarah is permitted to teach scripture to slaves on Sunday afternoons
Marries Halsey Fenimore Cooper
Moves to Chatanooga, Tennessee. Works as assistant editor for Halsey F. Cooper atThe Advertiser
Daughter Harriet (Hattie) born
Sister Harriet (Hattie) marries Julius Skilton
Sarah, Halsey, and Hattie flee north to Washington, D.C. Halsey works in Treasury Department in Internal Revenue Office
Daughter Mollie born
Sarah and her family return South to Memphis
Sarah organizes Bible Class for Union Soldiers
Sarah is president ofAssociation for Relief of White Refugees
Daughter Mollie dies
Sarah falls ill, travels around southern United States to find a more suitable climate
Sarah recuperates in St. Paul, Minnesota
Sarah, Halsey, and Hattie move to San Francisco; Sarah's health markedly improves
Halsey F. Cooper given job as Special Deputy Collector of Internal Revenue for district in San Francisco
Sarah and Hattie on cross-country trip
Sarah Cooper starts Jackson Street Kindergarten Association
Cooper School founded
Halsey F. Cooper fired from Internal Revenue Service
Heresy charge brought against Sarah Cooper by Deacon Roberts of the Calvary Presbyterian Church
Jackson Street Kindergarten Association becomes Golden Gate Kindergarten Association
Sarah's sister Jennie dies
Halsey F. Cooper commits suicide
Sarah writesA Refutation- a letter to John Morton, Surveyor of Internal Revenue Service, in defence of her husband
Sarah works extensively with Kindergartens, Women's Organizations, and her Bible Class.
Harriet Cooper suffers from Melancholia and Exhaustion
Sarah gives addresses at World's Columbian Exposition
Harriet Cooper's condition worsens
Sarah and Harriet Cooper die

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Personal material includes letters, Sarah and her daughter Harriet's diaries, calling cards, photographs, and daguerreotypes.

Included is correspondence of Ella Adams, Philip D. Armour, Rachel Foster Avery, Earl Barnes, Sophia L. Boardman, Nicholas E. Boyd, Lucy Brinkerhoff, Charles O. Browne, F.C. Clarke, Henry C. Dane, John Eaton, Nellie B. Eyster, Helen G. Fairchild, George T. Gaden, Minna V. Gaden, Caroline T. Haven, Phoebe Hearst, Ellen M. Henrotin, Robert G. Ingersoll, David Starr Jordan, Miranda W. Lux, Irena Ingersoll Rawlings, Emma B. Ryder, Caroline M. Severance, William E. Sheldon, Harriet Ingersoll Skilton, Charlotte Perkins Stetson (Gilman), Sarah A. Stewart, Ada van Pelt, E.G. Waite, and Joseph Warren.

Also includes material relevant to works and organizations that Sarah was involved in such as annual reports from Kindergarten Associations, lesson plans from Bible School classes, and programs from meetings of Women's Organizations.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: Frank A. Skilton papers, #1273. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Julius A. Skilton papers, #1273. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST

emph.ref: Series I. Correspondence and Personal Material, 1813-1921

arrangement.p: General correspondence between Sarah Cooper and her family and friends as well as letters from Susan B. Anthony, Frances Willard, Anna Howard Shaw, and Robert G. Ingersoll. Also letters and material concerning Sarah Cooper's involvement in the Reverend Brown trial and her own Heresy Trial. Diaries of Sarah Cooper and her daughter Harriet and biographical sketches of Sarah.
Manuscript of The Bankrupt's Daughter, a novel by Sarah Cooper; essays; articles; speeches.
Lessons from Sarah Cooper's Bible Classes; Annual Reports from the Golden Gate Kindergarten Association; essays on Kindergartens and Education by Sarah Cooper and others
includes photographs and daguerreotypes of Sarah Cooper, her family, friends, kindergartens.
Newspaper clippings by or related to Sarah Cooper.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Sarah Brown Ingersoll Cooper Papers, #6543. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Women social reformers.
Women -- Diaries.
Temperance.
Sunday schools.
Social reformers.
Social problems.
Presbyterian Church.
Kindergarten.
Educators.
Depression, Mental.
controlaccess.corpname: Golden Gate Kindergarten Association of San Francisco

did.unittitle: Series I. Correspondence and Personal Material
Correspondence and Personal Material: To Sarah from Halsey F. Cooper prior to marriage;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letter from Civil War Prisoner of War to Sarah; Confederate Postage Stamps;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters of condolence after the death of Sarah's daughter Mollie and roses from her grave;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several newspaper clippings about Radical Republicans of the time;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several rejection letters from publishing companies for Sarah's manuscripts;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several rejection letters from publishing companies for Sarah's manuscripts;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter commending Sarah on one of her magazine articles and another letter requesting her to write an article for a magazine;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes clippings and letters about the possible suicide of Mr. Ralston, President of the Bank of California and about the closing of the bank;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter about driving the Chinese out of California;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter to Sarah from U.S. Commissioner of Education John Eaton about the removal of federal troops from the South and the end of Reconstruction;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters from Chinese children in San Francisco to their teachers;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters from Chinese children in San Francisco to their teachers;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter concerning missionaries in Peking who translated the Old Testament into Chinese; letters from John Eaton about the possibility of Sarah teaching Home Economics to women in California; letters from John Eaton about the education reports of 1875 and 1876,

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes correspondence between Sarah and the Internal Revenue Service regarding Halsey Cooper's position as General Deputy; includes letter notifying Halsey Cooper of his dismissal from his position in Internal Revenue Service;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from F.C. Clarke concerning job prospects for Halsey Cooper and Sarah's Kindergarten work;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters from F.C. Clarke regarding secret arrangements being made for Halsey Cooper's job appointment;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters from F.C. Clarke regarding Halsey Cooper's appointment; letters from John Eaton to Sarah about various projects in education;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from F.C. Clarke regarding the nomination of John Morton for the position of Collector of Internal Revenue;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a certificate appointing Halsey Cooper as a Special Deputy Surveyor of Customs,
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters in reference to the accusations of heresy made against Sarah;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes the preamble and resolution regarding Sarah's heresy trial and the decision to allow her to continue teaching Bible classes; letters of support for Sarah during the trial;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters of support for Sarah during the heresy trial;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters of support for Sarah, a statement from John Eaton, and a letter from the New York Observer regarding her heresy trial;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from John Eaton;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from John Eaton;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from John Eaton;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from John Eaton;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from John Eaton;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from John Eaton;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letters from John Eaton about the possibility of Sarah making a trip to the East in the summer; includes several letters from Emily Faithfull, a British feminist and publisher;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Emily Faithfull about her visit to San Francisco; includes several letters from John Eaton about the passage of a bill which would establish common schools for American children of all races;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from John Eaton about the passage of a bill which would establish common schools for American children of all races and about the possiblity of calling Sarah's association The Cooper Kindergarten Association;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Halsey Cooper to the Governor in regards to the retention of his job through the change of administration; includes letters from Mr. Morton concerning his nomination of Mr. Brown to replace Mr. Cooper as Deputy Surveyor;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Halsey Cooper to President Grover Cleveland outlining his charges against Mr. Morton and a letter from Helen Fairchild regarding this letter;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from First Lady Elizabeth Cleveland regarding one of Sarah Cooper's papers on Kindergartens; includes a letter from John Eaton regarding a paper by Sarah presented at the Conference of Charities and Corrections;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several sympathy letters after the death of Sarah's sister;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from M.A. Cooper about the Cooper family and their relation to James Fenimore Cooper;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Halsey Cooper to his mother on his 57th birthday and his suicide note;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes sympathy letters to Sarah and Harriet and the account book of funds raised for Sarah and Halsey Cooper's mortgage;
A Refutation- letter from Sarah Cooper to John Morton, Surveyor of Internal Revenue Service, in defence of her husband

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Emily Faithfull encouraging Sarah to write to her; includes a letter from Sarah to Halsey Cooper on their thirty-first wedding anniversary;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Jane Stanford regarding a letter from Susan B. Anthony;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from William Sheldon which addresses the national and international influences of Sarah's Kindergarten work;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a printed copy of endorsements of the California Educational Review, including one by Sarah; letter from John Eaton;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a notice regarding Sarah's appointment to the Advisory Council of the Women's Branch of the World's Congress Auxiliary;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter about opening a kindergarten in British Columbia;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Rachel Foster Avery in regards to the International Kindergarten Union joining the National Council of Women;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Rachel Foster Avery in regards to the International Kindergarten Union joining the National Council of Women;,

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter about Sarah giving a talk on Public Schools and the Kindergarten System at the World's Congress of Representatitve Women; includes several letters from Sarah Stewart, Vice President of the International Kindergarten Union;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from California Secretary of State E.G. Waite about the Women's Suffrage Bill and the bill to pension teachers; includes two letters from Sarah Stewart;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from May Wright Sewall in regards to the building space given to the National Council of Women; includes a letter from Sarah Stewart; includes several letters from Rachel Foster Avery;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters about Sarah speaking at a chapel service at the University of Chicago; includes several letters from Philip D. Armour - a childhood friend of Sarah's from Cazenovia and head of the Armour Mission and Institute, the Armour Glue Works and various Railroad lines;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Armour and Company and a check for $250 and several letters from Philip D. Armour;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Philip D. Armour;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes letter from May Wright Sewall about getting a signed picture of Sarah for her photo collection;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Sarah to Philip D. Armour concerning Sarah's desire to start an institution similar to the Armour Institute and a request for him to invest money in her projects;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from the Armour Institute about Sarah's new school;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Philip D. Armour about his upcoming visit to San Francisco; includes a letter from Sarah Stewart; includes a letter from Anna Craig from Brigham Young Academy regarding kindergarten education in Utah;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters in regards to Sarah starting an institution similar to Hull House Association in San Francisco;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Rachel Foster Avery addressed to the women at the California Midwinter International Exposition;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from the Armour Institute; includes a letter from the Golden Gate Union requesting Sarah to speak; includes a letter from E.G. Waite regarding his political career; includes a letter from Charles F. Johnson in regards to Sarah writing an article for his upcoming book;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from H.J. McCoy in regards to Sarah giving an address at the opening of the San Francisco Y.M.C.A.;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from H.J. McCoy thanking Sarah for her address at the Y.M.C.A. and asking her to give another;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Philip D. Armour; includes a letter from Phoebe Hearst;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Philip D. Armour; includes a letter from Sarah Stewart;
Correspondence and Personal Material; includes a letter from Sarah Stewart;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from May Wright Sewall;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Philip D. Armour; includes a letter from Phoebe Hearst in which she refuses to pay Susan B. Anthony's expenses to the Pacific Coast Congress; includes a letter from Ada VanPelt about the busy schedule of Charlotte Perkins Stetson (Charlotte Perkins Gilman);
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Philip D. Armour;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Charlotte Perkins Stetson about her and Jane Addams' work with the Hull House Association;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Sarah to Harriet regarding Harriet's trip with Anna Howard Shaw; includes a letter inviting Sarah to dine with Adolph Sutro, the mayor of San Francisco;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Charlotte Perkins Stetson about Jane Addams and the Hull House Association;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Phoebe Hearst regarding her financial contributions to Sarah's kindergartens;
Correspondence and Personal Material: Letters from Lucy and Mary Anthony;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Phoebe Hearst;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Charlotte Perkins Stetson which discusses her latest trip and mentions Susan B. Anthony and Anna Howard Shaw; includes a letter about prostitutes in San Francisco who advertise in the daily paper; includes a letter from Rachel Foster Avery requesting an autographed picture of Sarah;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Phoebe Hearst;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Phoebe Hearst;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter concerning Americans condemned by the courts of South Africa; includes several letters from the Woman's Suffrage Association;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Mary Lowe Dickinson, President of the National Council of Women;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Philip D. Armour;
Correspondence and Personal Material: includes a letter from Sarah's sister Harriet Skilton expressing concern over Harriet Cooper's mental condition;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Sarah to her sister Harriet Skilton forshadowing her death; includes several letters from Harriet Skilton expressing concern about Harriet Cooper's condition;

Correspondence and Personal Material: includes several letters from Sarah to her sister Harriet Skilton forshadowing her death; includes several letters from Harriet Skilton expressing concern about Harriet Cooper's condition; includes a brief eulogy of Sarah;
Letters of Condolence to Ellen Henrotin, President of the General Federation of Women's Clubs,
Correspondence and Personal Material: including information on wills and the Cooper estate,
Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,
Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,
Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,
Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,
Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,
Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,
Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,

Correspondence and Personal Material - including information on wills and the Cooper estate,

Braided hair of Ellen, Emma, and Miss C. Mills

Robert Ingersoll essays and lectures:The Coming and Going Religion,misc
Robert Ingersoll essays and lectures:Mistakes of Ingersoll as shown by Professor Swing...,

Robert Ingersoll essays and lectures:Richard Ingersoll and Some of his Descendants,

Susan B. Anthony letters - Lucy and Mary Anthony
Sarah Cooper's Statement on the Brown affair
